questions.text: Photosynthesis is a physical-chemical process by which plants use light energy to drive the synthesis of:
questions.explanation: It converts solar energy into chemical energy stored in carbohydrates.

questions.text: Who showed that plants release oxygen only in the presence of sunlight?
questions.explanation: Ingenhousz used aquatic plants to show bubbles (O2) were formed only in light.

questions.text: The sites of light reaction and dark reaction in chloroplast are respectively:
questions.explanation: The membrane system (grana) is for light trapping; stroma is for enzymatic sugar synthesis.

questions.text: Which pigment is the 'reaction center' in both PS I and PS II?
questions.explanation: A single molecule of chlorophyll a forms the reaction center.

questions.text: The Z-scheme of light reaction occurs in:
options: Both PS I and PS II
questions.explanation: It involves the non-cyclic flow of electrons through both photosystems.

questions.text: The splitting of water during light reaction is associated with:
questions.explanation: Water splitting complex is located on the inner side of the thylakoid membrane associated with PS II.

questions.text: Cyclic photophosphorylation results only in the synthesis of:
questions.explanation: It involves only PS I and lacks NADP reductase.

questions.text: The Chemiosmotic Hypothesis explains the synthesis of ______ in chloroplasts.
questions.explanation: It involves the development of a proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane.

questions.text: The first stable product of the Calvin cycle (C3 pathway) is:
questions.explanation: C3 plants are named after this 3-carbon acid.

questions.text: The enzyme responsible for primary carboxylation in C3 plants is:
questions.explanation: Ribulose bisphosphate carboxylase-oxygenase is the most abundant enzyme.

questions.text: In C4 plants, the first stable product of CO2 fixation is:
questions.explanation: OAA is a 4-carbon acid, giving the pathway its name.

questions.text: C4 plants show a special leaf anatomy called:
questions.explanation: Bundle sheath cells form a 'wreath' around the vascular bundles.

questions.text: The C4 pathway avoids which wasteful process found in C3 plants?
questions.explanation: C4 plants lack photorespiration because they concentrate CO2 in bundle sheath cells.

questions.text: For every CO2 molecule entering the Calvin cycle, how many ATP and NADPH are required?
questions.explanation: 2 ATP/2 NADPH for reduction, 1 ATP for regeneration.

questions.text: To produce one molecule of glucose, how many turns of the Calvin cycle are needed?
questions.explanation: Each turn fixes one CO2; six turns fix the six carbons of glucose.

questions.text: Blackman's Law of ______ Factors states that the rate is determined by the factor at its sub-optimal level.
questions.explanation: If a process is influenced by more than one factor, the rate is limited by the slowest factor.

questions.text: Photorespiration occurs when RuBisCO binds with ______ instead of CO2.
questions.explanation: Binding with oxygen decreases photosynthetic yield.

questions.text: The primary CO2 acceptor in C4 plants is:
questions.explanation: PEP is present in the mesophyll cells of C4 plants.

questions.text: PS I has an absorption peak at ______ nm.
questions.explanation: Hence it is also called P700.

questions.text: PS II has an absorption peak at ______ nm.
questions.explanation: Hence it is also called P680.

questions.text: The oxygen evolved during photosynthesis comes from:
questions.explanation: Proven using radioisotope 18O by Ruben and Kamen.

questions.text: Which color of light is most effective for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Action spectrum shows peaks in blue and red regions.

questions.text: The enzyme PEP carboxylase is found in ______ cells of C4 plants.
questions.explanation: It fixes CO2 into OAA in the mesophyll cells.

questions.text: Bundle sheath cells are rich in which enzyme?
questions.explanation: They perform the Calvin cycle to produce sugars.

questions.text: What are the components of an LHC (Light Harvesting Complex)?
options: Hundreds of pigment molecules bound to proteins
questions.explanation: LHC gathers light and transfers it to the reaction center.

questions.text: Chlorophyll pigments are located in the:
questions.explanation: These membranes contain the photosystems and pigment molecules.

questions.text: Reaction involved in the formation of ATP from ADP using light is:
questions.explanation: Light energy drives the addition of inorganic phosphate to ADP.

questions.text: In C3 plants, CO2 compensation point is about:
questions.explanation: Compensation point is higher in C3 plants than C4 plants.

questions.text: What is the primary CO2 acceptor in C3 plants?
questions.explanation: Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate is a 5-carbon sugar.

questions.text: RuBisCO has a much greater affinity for ______ than for oxygen.
questions.explanation: However, it acts as an oxygenase when CO2 concentration is low or O2 is high.

questions.text: Non-cyclic photophosphorylation produces:
questions.explanation: Both high-energy compounds are needed for the dark reaction.

questions.text: The energy of light is captured by ______ molecules and funneled to reaction center.
questions.explanation: Antenna pigments include Chl b, xanthophylls, and carotenoids.

questions.text: Who discovered the biochemical pathway for CO2 fixation in C3 plants?
questions.explanation: Used radioactive 14C to trace the pathway (Calvin cycle).

questions.text: Saturation of photosynthesis with respect to CO2 occurs at ______ ppm for C3 plants.
questions.explanation: C4 plants saturate at about 360 ppm; C3 plants respond to higher levels.

questions.text: Magnesium is a key component of:
questions.explanation: The porphyrin ring of chlorophyll contains a central Mg atom.

questions.text: Light reaction is also known as:
questions.explanation: It includes light absorption, water splitting, and formation of ATP/NADPH.

questions.text: The Calvin cycle consists of three stages: carboxylation, reduction and ______.
questions.explanation: Regeneration of RuBP is essential for the cycle to continue.

questions.text: Photorespiration is initiated in:
questions.explanation: It involves a three-organelle pathway: Chloroplast -> Peroxisome -> Mitochondria.

questions.text: Most C4 plants are adapted to:
questions.explanation: They perform well at high temperatures and high light intensities.

questions.text: The process of ______ creates a proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane.
questions.explanation: Protons are moved into or produced in the lumen, while used in the stroma.
